name: Tudela de Duero — circular por la Senda del Duero (GR14)
region_intro: Jogging around Tudela De Duero offers a variety of running routes that explore the region's diverse landscape. The area is characterized by the meandering Duero River, providing scenic riverside paths, alongside gentle rolling hills and agricultural plains. These features create varied terrain suitable for different levels of running, from flat, easy jogging routes to more challenging ascents. Best jogging routes around Tudela De Duero The most popular jogging route is Sendero de la Atalaya (PRC-VA 49) — circular trailrunning desde Traspinedo, a 3.2 miles (5.1 km) trail that takes 38 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route offers trail running opportunities with some elevation gain. Another top favourite among local runners is Tudela de Duero — circular por la Senda del Duero (GR14), a difficult 9.3 miles (15.0 km) path. This route follows the Senda del Duero, offering extensive riverside scenery. Local runners also love the MTB/BMX Circuit loop from Despoblado de Fuentes de Duero, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail leading through an open circuit area, often completed in about 38 minutes. Jogging around Tudela De Duero is defined by riverside paths along the Duero, open agricultural landscapes, and gentle hills. The network offers options for various ability levels, including easy jogging routes and more advanced running trails. The routes in Tudela De Duero are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 2,700 runners have used komoot to explore Tudela De Duero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Tudela De Duero?
A: There are over 130 running routes around Tudela De Duero, offering a wide variety of options for all skill levels. The komoot community has explored these trails over 2,700 times.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Tudela De Duero?
A: Yes, Tudela De Duero offers several easy running routes perfect for beginners. You can find about 9 routes classified as easy, providing gentle terrain along the Duero River and through agricultural plains.

Q: What are some scenic running routes in Tudela De Duero?
A: Many routes in Tudela De Duero offer scenic views, particularly those following the Duero River. For extensive riverside scenery, consider the difficult 9.3-mile (15.0 km) route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120318489/tudela-de-duero-circular-por-la-senda-del-duero-gr14" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tudela de Duero — circular por la Senda del Duero (GR14)&lt;/a&gt;. The region's gentle rolling hills and open landscapes also provide picturesque backdrops for your run.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, circular routes are popular in Tudela De Duero. A notable option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120158955/sendero-de-la-atalaya-prc-va-49-circular-trailrunning-desde-traspinedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de la Atalaya (PRC-VA 49) — circular trailrunning desde Traspinedo&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 3.2-mile (5.1 km) trail offering trail running opportunities with some elevation gain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Tudela De Duero?
A: The terrain in Tudela De Duero is diverse, characterized by meandering riverside paths along the Duero, open agricultural landscapes, and gentle rolling hills. This variety provides options from flat, easy jogging routes to more challenging trails with elevation changes.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging running routes for advanced runners?
A: Absolutely. For advanced runners seeking a challenge, there are 14 difficult routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120318489/tudela-de-duero-circular-por-la-senda-del-duero-gr14" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tudela de Duero — circular por la Senda del Duero (GR14)&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 9.3-mile (15.0 km) path that follows the Senda del Duero, offering extensive riverside scenery.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Tudela De Duero?
A: The running routes in Tudela De Duero are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 20 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from riverside paths to open landscapes, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any points of interest or attractions near the running routes?
A: Yes, while jogging around Tudela De Duero, you can explore nearby attractions. These include historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4394423" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Valladolid&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5707746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María la Antigua&lt;/a&gt;, as well as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4154755" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera de Castilla Park&lt;/a&gt;. Many of these are easily accessible from various routes.

Q: Can I find running routes that are suitable for families?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, the region's easy and moderate routes, particularly those along the flat riverside paths, are generally suitable for families looking for a less strenuous run or walk together. Look for routes with minimal elevation gain.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by cafes or places to rest?
A: Many routes in the Tudela De Duero area are close to towns and villages where you can find cafes and amenities. While specific routes aren't highlighted for cafes, planning your run to start or end in Tudela De Duero or a nearby settlement will provide access to refreshments.

Q: What is a good time of year to go running in Tudela De Duero?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for running in Tudela De Duero, with milder temperatures. Summer can be hot, especially midday, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable. Winter can be cool but often still suitable for running, especially on clear days.

Q: Are there any lesser-known or 'hidden' running routes in Tudela De Duero?
A: While the guide highlights popular routes, the extensive network of over 130 trails means there are many less-trafficked paths to discover. Exploring the agricultural plains and smaller tracks away from the main riverside paths can reveal quieter, more 'hidden' running experiences.
tips: The Duero Trail in Tudela de Duero is a charming part of the GR-14 trail, also known as the Duero Natural Path. This trail is perfect for enjoying nature and the tranquility of the Duero River.
